name: Hoppel Poppel Tart
recipeIngredient: 4 cups Red Bliss potatoes, cut into -1/2-inch cubes (1 1/4 pounds)
1/4 cup Mayonnaise or creamy salad dressing
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. On a lightly floured surface, roll dough to a 14-inch round. Transfer to an 11-inch tart pan with removable bottom. Trim edges; prick bottom with the tines of a fork.
3. Line pastry shell with aluminum foil and fill with pastry weights, dried beans, or raw rice. Bake for 15 minutes.
4. Remove foil and weights. Bake for 5 to 6 minutes longer, or until the pastry starts to turn golden.
5. Brush with egg white; bake for 1 minute longer. Cool completely on a wire rack.
6. In a large skillet, heat oil over medium heat. Add sausage and mash with a fork into small pieces.
7. Add potatoes, onion, salt, and pepper. Cook until potatoes are fork tender, stirring frequently, about 20 minutes. Cool to room temperature.
8. Reduce oven temperature to 350°F. Stir dill and mayonnaise into the potato mixture; spoon into tart shell.
9. Bake for 30 minutes or until heated through. Cool 10 minutes on a wire rack before removing the outer ring.
10. Sprinkle with parsley. Serve warm.
Makes 8 servings.
